---
title: "GRAVE-D Protocol Master Dyadic Codebook v2.0"
project: "From Cooperation to Control: Authoritarian Leadership, Politics & International Conflict"
dataset_structure: "Directed Dyad-Year (Country A → Country B)"
unit_of_analysis: "The behavior of Sender (A) toward Target (B) in a given year."
last_updated: "March 1, 2026"
total_variables: 386
author: 
  - name: Tom Hanna
    id: th
    orcid: 0000-0002-8054-0335
    email: tlhanna@central.uh.edu
    url: tomhanna.me
    affiliation: 
      - name: University of Houston
        city: Houston
        state: TX
        url: uh.edu/class/political-science/
license: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0
copyright: 
  holder: Tom Hanna
  year: 2026
format:
  docx:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: true
    highlight-style: github
    page-width: 6.5
from: markdown-smart
execute:
  echo: true
  eval: false
  warning: false
  message: false
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    tidy: true
    tidy.opts:
      width.cutoff: 70
---


---

## 1. Identification Variables

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 1 | COWcode_a | Correlates of War | Integer | Sender ID. The country initiating the action or the primary focus of the observation. Derived from iso3a in the FBIC spine. |
| 2 | COWcode_b | Correlates of War | Integer | Target ID. The country receiving the action. Derived from iso3b in the FBIC spine. |
| 3 | year | Various | Integer | Observation year (1946–2020). |
| 4 | dyad | Calculated | Character | Undirected dyad identifier combining both COW codes. |
| 5 | unregiona | UN GeoScheme | Character | Geographic region of Country A (Sender). |
| 6 | unregionb | UN GeoScheme | Character | Geographic region of Country B (Target). |
| 27 | iso3a | COW / countrycode | Character | ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code for Country A. |
| 28 | iso3b | COW / countrycode | Character | ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code for Country B. |

---

## 2. FBIC Connectivity / Bandwidth (Dyadic Spine)

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 7 | bandwidth | FBIC | Continuous | Total Bandwidth. A composite index of total dyadic connectivity. Used to test the visibility of conflict signals. |
| 8 | economicbandwidth | FBIC | Continuous | Economic Bandwidth. Aggregation of trade volume, FDI flows, and economic agreements. |
| 9 | politicalbandwidth | FBIC | Continuous | Political Bandwidth. Measures diplomatic connectedness (embassy presence, shared IGO memberships). |
| 10 | securitybandwidth | FBIC | Continuous | Security Bandwidth. Measures military-to-military ties, defense pacts, and arms trade volume. |

> **Note:** `socialbandwidth` from v1.0 is not present in the final column list. If added in future versions it measures people-to-people exchanges, tourism, and migration flows.

Source: Foreign Bilateral Integration Capacity (FBIC) Dataset. These variables form the spine of the dataset and measure the "width of the pipe" between nations.

---

## 3. Conflict & Targeting Outcomes

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 11 | hihosta | MIDs v4.0 | Integer | Hostility Level (Sender). Highest hostility reached by Country A against Country B in that year. Scale: 0=None, 1=Threat, 2=Display, 3=Use of Force, 4=War. |
| 12 | mid_initiated | Calculated | Binary (0/1) | Conflict Initiation. Coded as 1 if hihosta ≥ 2. Indicates active revisionist behavior by the Sender. |
| 13 | targets_democracy | GRAVE-D | Binary (0/1) | Democratic Target Selection. Coded as 1 if Country B's v2x_libdem_b ≥ 0.5. |
| 14 | cold_war | Calculated | Binary (0/1) | Cold War period indicator. 1 if year is between 1947 and 1991 inclusive, 0 otherwise. |

Source: Derived from MIDs v4.0, V-Dem, and GRAVE-D feature engineering.

---

## 4. Revisionism & Ideology: The Will to Fight

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 15 | rev_potential_a | Calculated | Continuous | Revisionist Potential (Sender). A standardized composite index measuring the structural drive to disrupt the status quo. Arithmetic mean of Z-scores for: (1) Ideological Legitimation (v2exl_legitideol_a), (2) Autocratic Control (inverse of v2x_libdem_a), (3) Ideological Extremity (ideol_extremity_a). |
| 16 | rev_potential_b | Calculated | Continuous | Revisionist Potential (Target). Same construction as rev_potential_a, applied to Country B. |
| 17 | revisionism_distance | Calculated | Continuous | Revisionism Gap. The absolute difference in revisionist potential between the dyad. Formula: abs(rev_potential_a − rev_potential_b). |
| 18 | sidea_revisionist_domestic | V-Dem | Continuous | Domestic Revisionist Legitimation (Sender). Direct map from v2exl_legitideol_a. Higher values indicate stronger ideology-based legitimation. |
| 19 | sidea_nationalist_revisionist_domestic | Planned | NA | Nationalist sub-type of domestic revisionism. Placeholder (NA) — requires Colgan/manual classification. |
| 20 | sidea_socialist_revisionist_domestic | Planned | NA | Socialist sub-type. Placeholder (NA). |
| 21 | sidea_religious_revisionist_domestic | Planned | NA | Religious sub-type. Placeholder (NA). |
| 22 | sidea_reactionary_revisionist_domestic | Planned | NA | Reactionary sub-type. Placeholder (NA). |
| 23 | sidea_separatist_revisionist_domestic | Planned | NA | Separatist sub-type. Placeholder (NA). |
| 24 | sidea_dynamic_leader | V-Dem | Continuous | Personalist/charismatic leader legitimation (Sender). Direct map from v2exl_legitlead_a. |

### 4a. Support Coalition Variables

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 25 | sidea_religious_support | Planned | NA | Religious support base. Placeholder (NA) — needs WRP or manual coding. |
| 26 | sidea_party_elite_support | V-Dem | Continuous | Party/elite support (Sender). Power distributed by socioeconomic position. Maps from v2pepwrses_a. |
| 370 | sidea_ethnic_racial_support | V-Dem | Continuous | Ethnic/racial support (Sender). Power distributed by social group. Maps from v2pepwrsoc_a. |
| 371 | sidea_rural_worker_support | V-Dem | Continuous | Rural/worker support (Sender). Civil society participation. Maps from v2x_cspart_a. |
| 372 | sidea_military_support | Planned | NA | Military support base. Placeholder (NA) — no direct V-Dem proxy. |
| 373 | sidea_winning_coalition_size | V-Dem | Continuous | Winning coalition size proxy (Sender). Maps from v2dlreason_a (reasoned justification in deliberation). |

### 4b. Revisionist Potential Components

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 376 | global_med_ideol | Calculated | Continuous | Annual global median of v2exl_legitideol_a. Computed within year groups. |
| 377 | ideol_extremity_a | Calculated | Continuous | Ideological Extremity (Sender). abs(v2exl_legitideol_a − global_med_ideol). |
| 378 | ideol_extremity_b | Calculated | Continuous | Ideological Extremity (Target). abs(v2exl_legitideol_b − global_med_ideol). |
| 379 | dem_constraint_inv_a | Calculated | Continuous | Inverted Democratic Constraint (Sender). 1 − v2x_libdem_a. Higher = less constrained. |
| 380 | dem_constraint_inv_b | Calculated | Continuous | Inverted Democratic Constraint (Target). 1 − v2x_libdem_b. |
| 381 | z_legit_a | Calculated | Continuous | Z-score of v2exl_legitideol_a. |
| 382 | z_dem_a | Calculated | Continuous | Z-score of dem_constraint_inv_a. |
| 383 | z_ext_a | Calculated | Continuous | Z-score of ideol_extremity_a. |
| 384 | z_legit_b | Calculated | Continuous | Z-score of v2exl_legitideol_b. |
| 385 | z_dem_b | Calculated | Continuous | Z-score of dem_constraint_inv_b. |
| 386 | z_ext_b | Calculated | Continuous | Z-score of ideol_extremity_b. |

Source: Constructed in revisionist_potential logic (05_build_master.R) using V-Dem monadic inputs.

---

## 5. Temporal Controls

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 29 | peace_years | Calculated | Integer | Years since last MID onset in this dyad. |
| 30 | t | Calculated | Integer | Linear time counter for peace-years spline (Carter & Signorino 2010). |
| 31 | t2 | Calculated | Integer | Quadratic term: t². |
| 32 | t3 | Calculated | Integer | Cubic term: t³. |

---

## 6. Leader Data: Archigos

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 33 | obsid | Archigos | Character | Unique leader-spell observation ID. |
| 34 | leadid | Archigos | Character | Unique leader identifier (persistent across spells). |
| 35 | idacr | Archigos | Character | Country acronym identifier. |
| 36 | leader | Archigos | Character | Leader name. |
| 37 | startdate | Archigos | Date | Start date of leader tenure. |
| 38 | enddate | Archigos | Date | End date of leader tenure. |
| 39 | entry | Archigos | Character | Mode of entry to office (e.g., Regular, Irregular, Foreign). |
| 40 | exit | Archigos | Character | Mode of exit from office (e.g., Regular, Irregular, Death). |
| 41 | exitcode | Archigos | Character | Detailed exit classification code. |
| 42 | prevtimesinoffice | Archigos | Integer | Number of previous times this leader held office. |
| 43 | posttenurefate | Archigos | Character | Fate of leader after leaving office (e.g., OK, Exile, Prison, Death). |
| 44 | gender | Archigos | Character | Leader gender (M/F). |
| 45 | yrborn | Archigos | Integer | Leader birth year. |
| 46 | yrdied | Archigos | Integer | Leader death year (NA if still living). |
| 47 | borndate | Archigos | Date | Leader birth date. |
| 48 | deathdate | Archigos | Date | Leader death date (NA if still living). |
| 49 | dbpedia.uri | Archigos | Character | DBpedia URI link for the leader. |
| 50 | num.entry | Calculated | Integer | Numeric encoding of entry mode. |
| 51 | num.exit | Calculated | Integer | Numeric encoding of exit mode. |
| 52 | num.exitcode | Calculated | Integer | Numeric encoding of exit code. |
| 53 | num.posttenurefate | Calculated | Integer | Numeric encoding of post-tenure fate. |

Source: Archigos dataset (Goemans, Gleditsch & Chiozza). Merged in 03_build_grave_d_ideology.R.

---

## 7. Leader Data: Colgan Revolutionary Leaders

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 54 | fties | Colgan | Numeric | Foreign ties indicator. |
| 55 | ftcur | Colgan | Numeric | Current foreign ties. |
| 56 | stabb | Colgan | Character | State abbreviation (Colgan coding). |
| 57 | onsets | Colgan | Integer | Count of MID onsets during leader tenure. |
| 58 | revonsets | Colgan | Integer | Count of MID onsets by revolutionary leaders. |
| 59 | sideaonsets | Colgan | Integer | Count of Side A (initiator) MID onsets. |
| 60 | force_onsets | Colgan | Integer | Count of MID onsets involving use of force. |
| 61 | force_revonsets | Colgan | Integer | Force onsets by revolutionary leaders. |
| 62 | force_sideaonsets | Colgan | Integer | Force onsets where country was Side A. |
| 63 | obsid_colgan | Colgan | Character | Colgan's leader-spell observation ID. |
| 64 | ccname | Colgan | Character | Country name (Colgan coding). |
| 65 | leader_colgan | Colgan | Character | Leader name (Colgan coding). |
| 66 | startdate_colgan | Colgan | Date | Leader start date (Colgan). |
| 67 | enddate_colgan | Colgan | Date | Leader end date (Colgan). |
| 68 | entry_colgan | Colgan | Character | Mode of entry (Colgan coding). |
| 69 | prevtimesinoffice_colgan | Colgan | Integer | Previous times in office (Colgan). |
| 70 | posttenurefate_colgan | Colgan | Character | Post-tenure fate (Colgan). |
| 71 | gender_colgan | Colgan | Character | Leader gender (Colgan). |
| 72 | age0 | Colgan | Integer | Age at start of tenure. |
| 73 | startobs | Colgan | Date/Integer | Start of observation window. |
| 74 | endobs | Colgan | Date/Integer | End of observation window. |
| 75 | age | Colgan | Integer | Leader age in observation year. |
| 76 | numld | Colgan | Integer | Number of leaders in country-year. |
| 77 | usedforce | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Leader used military force during tenure. |
| 78 | irregulartransition | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Leader came to power via irregular transition. |
| 79 | foundingleader | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Leader is a founding leader of the state/regime. |
| 80 | foreigninstall | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Leader installed by a foreign power. |
| 81 | radicalideology | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Leader holds radical ideological orientation. |
| 82 | democratizing | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Country undergoing democratization during tenure. |
| 83 | revolutionaryleader | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Leader classified as revolutionary (Colgan definition). |
| 84 | ambiguouscoding | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Coding is ambiguous or disputed. |

### 7a. Revolutionary Change Categories (Colgan)

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 85 | chg_executivepower | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Revolutionary change in executive power structure. |
| 86 | chg_politicalideology | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Revolutionary change in political ideology. |
| 87 | chg_nameofcountry | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Country name changed during revolution. |
| 88 | chg_propertyowernship | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Revolutionary change in property ownership rules. |
| 89 | chg_womenandethnicstatus | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Revolutionary change in women's/ethnic group status. |
| 90 | chg_religioningovernment | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Revolutionary change in religion's role in government. |
| 91 | chg_revolutionarycommittee | Colgan | Binary (0/1) | Revolutionary committee established. |
| 92 | totalcategorieschanged | Colgan | Integer | Total count of revolutionary change categories (0–7). |
| 93 | revage | Colgan | Integer | Years since revolution at observation. |
| 94 | radrestrict | Colgan | Binary/Integer | Radical ideology with restricted coding. |

Source: Colgan (2012) "Measuring Revolution" dataset. Merged in 03_build_grave_d_ideology.R.

---

## 8. Leader Ideology & Polity Controls

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 95 | start_year | Leader Ideology / Polity | Integer | Start year of leader or polity observation. |
| 96 | code | Polity | Integer | Polity country code. |
| 97 | democ | Polity | Integer | Polity democracy score (0–10). |
| 98 | autoc | Polity | Integer | Polity autocracy score (0–10). |
| 99 | polity2 | Polity | Integer | Combined Polity score (−10 to +10). democ − autoc, with special codes converted. |
| 100 | durable | Polity | Integer | Regime durability. Years since last regime transition. |
| 101 | polity | Polity | Integer | Raw Polity score before special code conversion. |
| 102 | muslim | Leader Ideology | Continuous | Muslim population share (from leader ideology dataset). |
| 103 | flgdpen | Leader Ideology | Continuous | Log GDP per capita (from leader ideology dataset). |
| 104 | flethfrac | Leader Ideology | Continuous | Ethnic fractionalization index. |
| 105 | region | Leader Ideology | Integer/Character | Regional classification. |
| 106 | leg_british | Leader Ideology | Binary (0/1) | British legal tradition. |
| 107 | leg_french | Leader Ideology | Binary (0/1) | French legal tradition. |
| 108 | leg_socialist | Leader Ideology | Binary (0/1) | Socialist legal tradition. |
| 109 | leg_german | Leader Ideology | Binary (0/1) | German legal tradition. |
| 110 | leg_scandivanian | Leader Ideology | Binary (0/1) | Scandinavian legal tradition. |
| 111 | borders | Leader Ideology | Integer | Number of land borders. |
| 112 | tpop | Leader Ideology | Numeric | Total population (from leader ideology dataset). |
| 113 | cinc | Leader Ideology | Continuous | CINC score (from leader ideology dataset, monadic). |
| 114 | nonrevonsets | Leader Ideology | Integer | Non-revolutionary MID onsets. |
| 115 | oecd | Leader Ideology | Binary (0/1) | OECD membership indicator. |
| 116 | majorpower | Leader Ideology | Binary (0/1) | Major power status (COW definition). |
| 117 | state_num | Leader Ideology | Integer | State number identifier. |
| 118 | regnum | Leader Ideology | Integer | Regime number identifier. |
| 119 | coldwar | Leader Ideology | Binary (0/1) | Cold War indicator (from leader ideology dataset). |
| 120 | uniqtag | Leader Ideology | Character | Unique tag for leader-country-year. |
| 121 | tenure | Leader Ideology | Integer | Leader tenure in years at observation. |
| 122 | peaceyears | Leader Ideology | Integer | Peace years (from leader ideology dataset). |
| 123 | entcat | Leader Ideology | Integer/Character | Entry category classification. |
| 124 | entrc | Leader Ideology | Binary (0/1) | Entry via revolutionary change. |
| 125 | entrcage | Leader Ideology | Integer | Age at revolutionary entry. |
| 126 | entrc15 | Leader Ideology | Binary (0/1) | Revolutionary entry within 15 years. |
| 127 | revstart | Leader Ideology | Integer | Year revolution started. |
| 128 | durrc | Leader Ideology | Integer | Duration since revolutionary change. |
| 129 | durrcage | Leader Ideology | Integer | Duration-age interaction for revolutionary change. |
| 130 | durrc15 | Leader Ideology | Binary (0/1) | Revolutionary change within 15 years. |
| 131 | dem | Leader Ideology | Binary (0/1) | Democracy indicator (from leader ideology dataset). |
| 132 | seveninstitutionscoded | Leader Ideology | Binary (0/1) | All seven institutional categories coded. |

Source: Global Leader Ideology dataset and Polity project. Merged in 03_build_grave_d_ideology.R.

---

## 9. V-Dem Indicators (Suffixed _a for Sender, _b for Target)

All V-Dem variables are merged from the `vdemdata` R package in 04_build_controls.R. Each variable appears twice — once with `_a` suffix (Country A / Sender) and once with `_b` suffix (Country B / Target). Source: V-Dem v15 (Coppedge et al. 2025).

### 9a. Democracy & Regime Indices

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Scale | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------|-------------|
| 133/236 | v2x_polyarchy | 0–1 interval | Electoral Democracy Index. Measures the extent to which the ideal of electoral democracy is achieved. |
| 134/237 | v2x_libdem | 0–1 interval | Liberal Democracy Index. Electoral democracy plus individual liberties and checks on executive power. |
| 135/238 | v2x_partipdem | 0–1 interval | Participatory Democracy Index. Active citizen participation in political processes. |
| 136/239 | v2x_delibdem | 0–1 interval | Deliberative Democracy Index. Public reasoning focused on the common good motivates political decisions. |
| 137/240 | v2x_egaldem | 0–1 interval | Egalitarian Democracy Index. Rights, resources, and power distributed equally across social groups. |
| 138/241 | v2x_regime | Ordinal 0–3 | Regimes of the World. 0=Closed autocracy, 1=Electoral autocracy, 2=Electoral democracy, 3=Liberal democracy. |
| 139/242 | v2x_regime_amb | Ordinal 0–9 | Regimes of the World (Ambiguous). Finer-grained version with ambiguous categories between regime types. |

### 9b. Executive Access & Structure

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Scale | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------|-------------|
| 140/243 | v2x_ex_military | 0–1 interval | Military influence on executive. Degree to which the military controls executive appointment and policy. |
| 141/244 | v2x_ex_confidence | 0–1 interval | Executive legitimacy via confidence. Legislature can remove executive via vote of no confidence. |
| 142/245 | v2x_ex_direlect | 0–1 interval | Direct election of executive. Degree to which the executive is directly elected by popular vote. |
| 143/246 | v2x_ex_hereditary | 0–1 interval | Hereditary executive. Degree to which executive selection is hereditary. |
| 144/247 | v2x_ex_party | 0–1 interval | Party-based executive. Degree to which ruling party controls executive selection. |

### 9c. Neopatrimonialism & Clientelism

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Scale | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------|-------------|
| 145/248 | v2x_neopat | 0–1 interval | Neopatrimonialism Index. Extent to which the state apparatus is used for private gain. |
| 146/249 | v2xnp_client | 0–1 interval | Clientelism Index. Extent of particularistic exchanges between politicians and voters. |

### 9d. Civil Society & Deliberation

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Scale | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------|-------------|
| 147/250 | v2x_frassoc_thick | 0–1 interval | Thick Freedom of Association. Robust associational environment beyond minimalist standards. |
| 148/251 | v2x_cspart | 0–1 interval | Civil Society Participation Index. Are major CSOs consulted by policymakers; how large is public involvement in CSOs. |
| 149/252 | v2dlreason | Interval | Reasoned Justification. Extent to which political elites give reasoned justification for positions. |
| 150/253 | v2dlcommon | Interval | Common Good Justification. Extent to which elites justify positions in terms of the common good. |
| 151/254 | v2dlcountr | Interval | Respect for Counterarguments. Extent to which elites acknowledge and respect opposing views. |
| 152/255 | v2dlencmps | Interval | Particularistic vs. Public Goods. Whether policy outputs target narrow groups or provide public goods. |

### 9e. Institutional Constraints

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Scale | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------|-------------|
| 153/256 | v2x_jucon | 0–1 interval | Judicial Constraints on Executive. Extent to which the judiciary can constrain executive action. |
| 154/257 | v2xlg_legcon | 0–1 interval | Legislative Constraints on Executive. Extent to which the legislature can question, investigate, and oversee the executive. |
| 155/258 | v2juhcind | Interval | High Court Independence. Independence of the highest court from executive influence. |
| 156/259 | v2juncind | Interval | Lower Court Independence. Independence of lower courts from executive influence. |
| 157/260 | v2juhccomp | Interval | High Court Compliance. Extent to which the government complies with high court decisions. |
| 158/261 | v2jucomp | Interval | Compliance with Judiciary. General government compliance with judicial decisions. |
| 159/262 | v2jureview | Interval | Judicial Review. Extent to which courts can invalidate unconstitutional government actions. |

### 9f. Corruption, Rule of Law & Civil Liberties

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Scale | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------|-------------|
| 160/263 | v2x_corr | 0–1 interval | Political Corruption Index. Higher values = more corruption. |
| 161/264 | v2x_rule | 0–1 interval | Rule of Law Index. Transparency, predictability, and equality of law enforcement. |
| 162/265 | v2xcl_prpty | Interval | Property Rights. Government respect for property rights. |
| 163/266 | v2x_gencl | 0–1 interval | General Civil Liberties Index. |
| 164/267 | v2x_clpol | 0–1 interval | Political Civil Liberties. Freedom of political expression and association. |
| 165/268 | v2x_clpriv | 0–1 interval | Private Civil Liberties. Freedom of movement, religion, property. |
| 166/269 | v2xcs_ccsi | 0–1 interval | Core Civil Society Index. CSO autonomy and citizen engagement. |

### 9g. Legislature

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Scale | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------|-------------|
| 167/270 | v2lgoppart | Interval | Legislature Opposition Parties. Ability of opposition parties to exercise oversight. |

### 9h. State Capacity & Territory

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Scale | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------|-------------|
| 168/271 | v2stfisccap | Interval | State Fiscal Capacity. Government ability to tax and spend effectively. |
| 169/272 | v2svstterr | Interval | State Authority over Territory. Extent of effective government control over sovereign territory. |

### 9i. Media

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Scale | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------|-------------|
| 170/273 | v2mecenefi | Interval | Government Censorship Effort — Internet. Extent of government effort to censor online media. |
| 171/274 | v2mecenefm | Interval | Government Censorship Effort — Media. Extent of government effort to censor print/broadcast media. |

### 9j. Ideology-Based Legitimation

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Scale | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------|-------------|
| 172/275 | v2exl_legitideol | Interval | Ideological Legitimation. To what extent does the leader justify their rule using ideology? Base variable for rev_potential. |
| 173/276 | v2exl_legitperf | Interval | Performance Legitimation. Leader justifies rule through economic or governance performance. |
| 174/277 | v2exl_legitlead | Interval | Personalist/Charismatic Legitimation. Leader justifies rule through personal charisma or personality cult. |
| 175/278 | v2exl_legitratio | Interval | Rational-Legal Legitimation. Leader justifies rule through established legal/procedural norms. |

### 9k. Ideology Type Scores

These are continuous latent variable scores measuring the extent to which each ideology type is used for regime legitimation. Produced by V-Dem's Bayesian item response theory measurement model. Higher values indicate greater reliance on that ideology type. Source: V-Dem v15, variable v2exl_legitideolcr.

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------------|
| 176/279 | v2exl_legitideolcr_0 | Nationalist ideology. Appeals to nationalism, national greatness, or ethnic/cultural identity as the basis for regime legitimation. |
| 177/280 | v2exl_legitideolcr_1 | Socialist or communist ideology. Appeals to Marxism-Leninism, socialism, or class-based revolutionary ideology. |
| 178/281 | v2exl_legitideolcr_2 | Restorative or conservative ideology. Appeals to restoring a previous political order or preserving the status quo. |
| 179/282 | v2exl_legitideolcr_3 | Separatist or autonomist ideology. Appeals to self-determination, regional independence, or political autonomy from a larger state or imperial structure. |
| 180/283 | v2exl_legitideolcr_4 | Religious ideology. Appeals to religious doctrine, divine authority, or theocratic governance. |


### 9l. Power Distribution

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Scale | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------|-------------|
| 181/284 | v2pepwrses | Interval | Power Distributed by Socioeconomic Position. Wealthy vs. poor influence on politics. |
| 182/285 | v2pepwrsoc | Interval | Power Distributed by Social Group. Dominant vs. marginalized group political influence. |
| 183/286 | v2xpe_exlsocgr | Interval | Exclusion by Social Group Index. Degree of political exclusion by social identity. |

### 9m. Head of State / Government

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Scale | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------|-------------|
| 184/287 | v2exhoshog | Binary 0/1 | HOS = HOG. Is the Head of State also Head of Government? |
| 185/288 | v2exremhog | Interval | HOG Removal by Legislature. Could the legislature successfully remove the HOG? |
| 186/289 | v2exdjdshg | Interval | HOG Dissolution Power. Could the HOG dissolve the legislature? |
| 187/290 | v2exdfvthg | Interval | HOG Veto Power. Could the HOG successfully veto legislation? |

### 9n. Regime Support Groups Scale

Each variable indicates the importance that the regime relies on a particular support group. Source: V-Dem Regime survey.

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Group |
|---------|--------------|-------|
| 188/291 | v2regsupgroups_0 | Aristocracy / hereditary elites |
| 189/292 | v2regsupgroups_1 | Agrarian elites / large landholders |
| 190/293 | v2regsupgroups_2 | Party elites |
| 191/294 | v2regsupgroups_3 | Business elites |
| 192/295 | v2regsupgroups_4 | State bureaucracy / civil servants |
| 193/296 | v2regsupgroups_5 | Military |
| 194/297 | v2regsupgroups_6 | Ethnic or racial group |
| 195/298 | v2regsupgroups_7 | Religious group |
| 196/299 | v2regsupgroups_8 | Local elites / chiefs |
| 197/300 | v2regsupgroups_9 | Urban working classes / labor unions |
| 198/301 | v2regsupgroups_10 | Urban middle classes |
| 199/302 | v2regsupgroups_11 | Rural working classes / peasants |
| 200/303 | v2regsupgroups_12 | Rural middle classes / family farmers |
| 201/304 | v2regsupgroups_13 | Foreign government / colonial power |

### 9o. Regime Support Metadata

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Scale | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------|-------------|
| 202/305 | v2regsupgroupssize | Interval | Total size of regime support base (% of adult population). |
| 203/306 | v2regimpgroup | Nominal 0–13 | Most important support group (mode of coder responses). Maps to v2regsupgroups categories. |
| 204/307 | v2reginfo | Text/Nominal | Regime name and start/end dates (pre-coded identifier). |
| 205/308 | v2regint | Binary 0/1 | Regime information available. 0 = no regime information for this country-date. |
| 206/309 | v2regendtype | Ordinal 0–7 | Regime End Type. 0=Military coup, 1=Other coup, 2=Self-coup, 3=Assassination, 4=Natural death, 5=Resignation, 6=Institutional change, 7=Foreign intervention. |

### 9p. HOG Removal Scale (0–8)

Influence that each body has on  successfully removing the Head of Government.

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Body |
|---------|--------------|------|
| 207/310 | v2exrmhgnp_0 | None |
| 208/311 | v2exrmhgnp_1 | Foreign power |
| 209/312 | v2exrmhgnp_2 | Ruling party leadership |
| 210/313 | v2exrmhgnp_3 | Royal council |
| 211/314 | v2exrmhgnp_4 | Military |
| 212/315 | v2exrmhgnp_5 | Head of State |
| 213/316 | v2exrmhgnp_6 | Religious body |
| 214/317 | v2exrmhgnp_7 | Tribal or ethnic council |
| 215/318 | v2exrmhgnp_8 | Other |

### 9q. HOG Control / Approval Scale (0–8)

Scale of HOG seeks prior approval from each body for domestic policy.

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Body |
|---------|--------------|------|
| 216/319 | v2exctlhg_0 | None |
| 217/320 | v2exctlhg_1 | Foreign power |
| 218/321 | v2exctlhg_2 | Ruling party leadership |
| 219/322 | v2exctlhg_3 | Royal council |
| 220/323 | v2exctlhg_4 | Military |
| 221/324 | v2exctlhg_5 | Head of State |
| 222/325 | v2exctlhg_6 | Religious body |
| 223/326 | v2exctlhg_7 | Tribal or ethnic council |
| 224/327 | v2exctlhg_8 | Other |

### 9r. V-Dem Economic & Social Variables (e_ prefix)

These are external variables distributed with V-Dem. Names may vary across vdemdata package versions.

| # (a/b) | Variable stem | Scale | Description |
|---------|--------------|-------|-------------|
| 225/328 | epop | Numeric | Population (thousands). Current V-Dem name; formerly e_mipopula. |
| 226/329 | e_cow_exports | Numeric | COW Exports. Total national exports in current dollars (COW trade data). |
| 227/330 | e_cow_imports | Numeric | COW Imports. Total national imports in current dollars (COW trade data). |
| 228/331 | e_total_fuel_income_pc | Numeric | Total Fuel Income Per Capita. Oil, gas, and coal income per capita (constant USD). |
| 229/332 | e_total_oil_income_pc | Numeric | Total Oil Income Per Capita. Oil-only income per capita (constant USD). |
| 230/333 | e_miurbani | Numeric | Urbanization. Percentage of population in urban areas. May be absent in newer vdemdata versions. |
| 231/334 | e_civil_war | Binary (0/1) | Civil War. Indicator for ongoing civil war in that country-year. |
| 232/335 | e_miinteco | Ordinal | Interstate Conflict Status. Level of interstate military conflict involvement. |
| 233/336 | e_legparty | Ordinal | Legal Parties. Number/status of legal political parties. |
| 234/337 | e_autoc | Numeric | Autocracy indicator (external to V-Dem, distributed with dataset). |
| 235/338 | e_peaveduc | Numeric | Average Education. Average years of education in the adult population. |

---

## 10. Material Capabilities (CINC)

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 339 | cinc_a | COW NMC | Continuous | Composite Index of National Capability (Sender). Aggregates military expenditure, military personnel, energy consumption, iron/steel production, urban population, and total population. Scale: 0–1 (share of global capabilities). |
| 340 | cinc_b | COW NMC | Continuous | Composite Index of National Capability (Target). Same construction as cinc_a. |

Source: COW National Material Capabilities v5+. Merged in 04_build_controls.R.

---

## 11. Alliance Data (ATOP)

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 341 | ddyad | ATOP v5.1 | Integer | ATOP directed dyad identifier. |
| 342 | atopally | ATOP v5.1 | Binary (0/1) | Any active alliance in this dyad-year. |
| 343 | defense | ATOP v5.1 | Binary (0/1) | Active defense pact. Signatories obliged to intervene militarily if partner attacked. |
| 344 | offense | ATOP v5.1 | Binary (0/1) | Active offense pact. Signatories obliged to cooperate in aggressive military action. |
| 345 | neutral | ATOP v5.1 | Binary (0/1) | Active neutrality pact. |
| 346 | nonagg | ATOP v5.1 | Binary (0/1) | Active non-aggression pact. |
| 347 | consul | ATOP v5.1 | Binary (0/1) | Active consultation pact. Obligation to consult in times of crisis. |
| 348 | shareob | ATOP v5.1 | Binary (0/1) | Shared obligations beyond the five core types. |
| 349 | transyr | ATOP v5.1 | Integer | Year of alliance transition (formation, termination, or change). |
| 350 | bilatno | ATOP v5.1 | Integer | Bilateral alliance count. Number of bilateral alliances active in this dyad-year. |
| 351 | multino | ATOP v5.1 | Integer | Multilateral alliance count. Number of multilateral alliances active in this dyad-year. |
| 352 | number | ATOP v5.1 | Integer | Total alliance count. |
| 353 | asymm | ATOP v5.1 | Binary (0/1) | Asymmetric obligations. Alliance imposes different obligations on the two parties. |
| 354–360 | atopid1–atopid7 | ATOP v5.1 | Integer | Alliance treaty identifiers. ATOP numeric IDs for up to 7 concurrent active alliances. |
| 361 | version | ATOP v5.1 | Character/Numeric | ATOP dataset version number. |

Source: Alliance Treaty Obligations and Provisions v5.1 (Leeds et al.). Merged in 04_build_controls.R as directed dyad-year.

---

## 12. Religious Demographics (WRP)

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 362 | chrstgenpct_a | WRP | Continuous (0–100) | Christian Percentage (Sender). Total percentage of Christian adherents. |
| 363 | islmgenpct_a | WRP | Continuous (0–100) | Muslim Percentage (Sender). Total percentage of Muslim adherents. |
| 364 | budgenpct_a | WRP | Continuous (0–100) | Buddhist Percentage (Sender). |
| 365 | hindgenpct_a | WRP | Continuous (0–100) | Hindu Percentage (Sender). |
| 366 | chrstgenpct_b | WRP | Continuous (0–100) | Christian Percentage (Target). |
| 367 | islmgenpct_b | WRP | Continuous (0–100) | Muslim Percentage (Target). |
| 368 | budgenpct_b | WRP | Continuous (0–100) | Buddhist Percentage (Target). |
| 369 | hindgenpct_b | WRP | Continuous (0–100) | Hindu Percentage (Target). |

### 12a. Religious Distance (Calculated)

| # | Variable | Source | Type | Description |
|---|----------|--------|------|-------------|
| 374 | islm_dist | Calculated | Continuous | Islamic Distance. abs(islmgenpct_a − islmgenpct_b). Difference in Muslim population share between the dyad. |
| 375 | chrst_dist | Calculated | Continuous | Christian Distance. abs(chrstgenpct_a − chrstgenpct_b). Difference in Christian population share. |

Source: COW World Religion Project (Maoz & Henderson 2013). WRP data is released in 5-year intervals (1945–2010). Interpolation and imputation strategies as described in the GRAVE-M codebook. Merged in 04_build_controls.R.

---

## Appendix A: Variable Count Summary

| Section | Variables | Count |
|---------|-----------|-------|
| 1. Identification | COWcode_a/b, year, dyad, unregion, iso3 | 8 |
| 2. FBIC Bandwidth | bandwidth, economic/political/security | 4 |
| 3. Conflict & Targeting | hihosta, mid_initiated, targets_democracy, cold_war | 4 |
| 4. Revisionism & Ideology | rev_potential, sidea_*, z-scores, components | 28 |
| 5. Temporal Controls | peace_years, t, t², t³ | 4 |
| 6. Archigos Leaders | obsid through dbpedia.uri | 21 |
| 7. Colgan Leaders | fties through seveninstitutionscoded | 79 |
| 8. Leader Ideology & Polity | start_year through seveninstitutionscoded | 38 |
| 9. V-Dem (×2 sides) | v2x_polyarchy through e_peaveduc (×2) | 206 |
| 10. CINC | cinc_a, cinc_b | 2 |
| 11. ATOP Alliances | ddyad through version | 21 |
| 12. Religion (WRP) | chrstgenpct through chrst_dist | 10 |
| **Total** | | **386** |

---

## Appendix B: Data Sources

| Source | Citation | URL |
|--------|----------|-----|
| FBIC | Kadera & Sorokin | — |
| MIDs v4.0 | Palmer et al. (2022) | correlatesofwar.org |
| V-Dem v15 | Coppedge et al. (2025) | v-dem.net |
| Archigos | Goemans, Gleditsch & Chiozza | archigos.org |
| Colgan | Colgan (2012) "Measuring Revolution" | — |
| Global Leader Ideology | — | — |
| Polity | Marshall & Gurr | systemicpeace.org |
| COW NMC | Singer, Bremer & Stuckey | correlatesofwar.org |
| ATOP v5.1 | Leeds et al. | atop-data.com |
| WRP | Maoz & Henderson (2013) | correlatesofwar.org |
| FUVF | Caprioli & Trumbore | d.umn.edu/~mcapriol/ |
| countrycode | Arel-Bundock | CRAN |

---

## Appendix C: Processing Notes

1. **Directed dyad structure.** All monadic variables (V-Dem, CINC, WRP, Archigos, leader data) are merged twice: once for Side A (Sender, `_a` suffix) and once for Side B (Target, `_b` suffix).

2. **V-Dem version handling.** The pipeline uses `any_of()` in the V-Dem `select()` call to handle variable name changes across vdemdata package versions. Old names (`e_migdppc`, `e_mipopula`) are harmonized to current names (`egdppc`, `epop`) post-select.

3. **WRP interpolation.** WRP data is released at 5-year intervals. Intervening years use linear interpolation; post-2010 years use Last Observation Carried Forward (LOCF).

4. **Revisionist potential.** Constructed from three Z-score-standardized components: ideological legitimation (`v2exl_legitideol`), inverted liberal democracy (`1 − v2x_libdem`), and ideological extremity (distance from annual global median). The composite is the arithmetic mean of the three Z-scores.

5. **Placeholder variables.** Several `sidea_*` sub-type variables (nationalist, socialist, religious, reactionary, separatist revisionism; military support; religious support) are currently coded as `NA_real_`. These require supplementary coding from Colgan classification or manual expert coding.

6. **Pipeline scripts.**
   - `01_build_fbic_spine.R` — FBIC bandwidth spine
   - `02_build_conflict.R` — MID conflict variables
   - `03_build_grave_d_ideology.R` — Archigos, Colgan, leader ideology
   - `04_build_controls.R` — V-Dem, CINC, ATOP, WRP, FUVF, economic controls
   - `05_build_master.R` — merge, revisionist potential, derived variables, export


---

## Appendix D: Imputation Protocol (06_impute_controls.R)

### Purpose

The GRAVE-D dataset contains scattered missing values in continuous V-Dem, CINC, and economic control variables due to differences in temporal and geographic coverage across source datasets. Script `06_impute_controls.R` applies a conservative, multi-stage imputation strategy to fill these gaps while preserving the integrity of structurally missing data.

### What Is NOT Imputed

The following variable categories are excluded from all imputation stages. Their missing values are structural — they reflect the absence of data coverage, not random gaps.

| Category | Examples | Reason |
|----------|----------|--------|
| Identifiers & keys | COWcode_a/b, year, dyad, iso3a/b | Complete by construction. |
| FBIC spine | bandwidth, economicbandwidth, etc. | Complete by construction. |
| Conflict outcomes | hihosta, mid_initiated, cold_war | Complete or structurally defined. |
| Temporal controls | peace_years, t, t², t³ | Complete by construction. |
| Archigos leader variables | obsid, leader, entry, exit, etc. | Missing = dyad-year outside Archigos coverage. Not a gap. |
| Colgan leader variables | revolutionaryleader, chg_*, revage, etc. | Missing = leader not in Colgan dataset or not a revolutionary leader. |
| ATOP treaty identifiers | ddyad, transyr, atopid1–7, version | Missing = no alliance exists. Binary alliance indicators (atopally, defense, etc.) are zero-filled separately. |
| Internet censorship | v2mecenefi_a/b | Structurally absent for the pre-internet era. Not a gap. |
| Variables with >80% missing | Any variable exceeding 80% NA | Insufficient observed data for reliable imputation. |
| Character/text variables | All non-numeric columns | Cannot be interpolated. |
| Derived variables | rev_potential_a/b, targets_democracy, sidea_*, islm_dist, etc. | Not imputed directly — recomputed in Stage 4 from imputed base variables. |

### Stage 1: Country-Year Panel Interpolation

**Method:** Linear interpolation within each country's time series using R's `approx()` function with `rule = 2`.

**Logic:** For each eligible numeric variable, the script extracts a monadic country-year panel (one row per country per year), groups by country, and interpolates between observed values. At the edges of each country's time series:
- Years **before** the first observation receive the earliest observed value (carried backward).
- Years **after** the last observation receive the latest observed value (carried forward — LOCF).

**Scope:** Applied separately to Side A variables (grouped by COWcode_a) and Side B variables (grouped by COWcode_b). This ensures that imputation respects the monadic structure — a value imputed for country X in year Y is consistent across all dyads involving country X in that year.

**Rationale:** Most V-Dem and economic variables change gradually within a country. Linear interpolation between observed values is the most defensible assumption for filling 1–3 year gaps within an otherwise observed time series.

### Stage 2: Regional-Year Median Fill

**Method:** For key V-Dem indices and CINC only, remaining missing values are filled with the median value from the same UN geographic region and year.

**Scope:** This stage only fires for countries with **no observed data at all** in the base variable — typically microstates or territories outside V-Dem's country coverage. Countries that had any observed data were already handled by Stage 1.

**Variables affected:** `v2x_polyarchy`, `v2x_libdem`, `v2x_corr`, `v2x_rule`, `v2exl_legitideol`, `v2exl_legitlead`, `v2x_cspart`, `v2pepwrses`, `v2pepwrsoc`, `v2dlreason`, `v2exl_legitideolcr_0` through `_4`, and `cinc` (all with `_a` and `_b` suffixes).

**Rationale:** Regional medians provide a weak but defensible prior for countries with zero coverage. This prevents listwise deletion of entire dyads involving microstates or non-covered territories in downstream analysis.

### Stage 3: Derived Variable Recomputation

After Stages 1–2, all derived variables are recomputed from their now-imputed base inputs. This ensures internal consistency — a change in an imputed `v2x_libdem_a` value propagates correctly to `targets_democracy`, `dem_constraint_inv_a`, `rev_potential_a`, and `revisionism_distance`.

| Derived variable | Recomputed from |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| sidea_revisionist_domestic | v2exl_legitideol_a |
| sidea_nationalist_revisionist_domestic | v2exl_legitideolcr_0_a |
| sidea_socialist_revisionist_domestic | v2exl_legitideolcr_1_a |
| sidea_reactionary_revisionist_domestic | v2exl_legitideolcr_2_a |
| sidea_separatist_revisionist_domestic | v2exl_legitideolcr_3_a |
| sidea_religious_revisionist_domestic | v2exl_legitideolcr_4_a |
| sidea_dynamic_leader | v2exl_legitlead_a |
| sidea_party_elite_support | v2pepwrses_a |
| sidea_ethnic_racial_support | v2pepwrsoc_a |
| sidea_rural_worker_support | v2x_cspart_a |
| sidea_military_support | cinc_a |
| sidea_religious_support | v2regsupgroups_7_a |
| sidea_winning_coalition_size | v2dlreason_a |
| targets_democracy | v2x_libdem_a < 0.3 AND v2x_libdem_b ≥ 0.5 |
| ideol_extremity_a/b | abs(v2exl_legitideol − annual global median) |
| dem_constraint_inv_a/b | 1 − v2x_libdem |
| z_legit_a/b, z_dem_a/b, z_ext_a/b | Z-scores of above components |
| rev_potential_a/b | (z_legit + z_dem + z_ext) / 3 |
| revisionism_distance | abs(rev_potential_a − rev_potential_b) |
| islm_dist | abs(islmgenpct_a − islmgenpct_b) |
| chrst_dist | abs(chrstgenpct_a − chrstgenpct_b) |

### Stage 4: Export and Diagnostics

The script overwrites `data/GRAVE_D_Master.rds` and `ready_data/GRAVE_D_Master.csv` with the imputed versions and prints a diagnostic table showing remaining NA counts and percentages for key analysis variables.

**Note:** The `GRAVE_D_Master_with_Leaders.csv` export (which adds Side B leader data) must be regenerated after imputation by re-running the leader merge block from `05_build_master.R` or appending it to the end of `06_impute_controls.R`.

### Remaining NAs in key analysis variables

v2x_libdem_a                                     59198  ( 3.5%)
    v2x_libdem_b                                     59198  ( 3.5%)
    v2exl_legitideol_a                               59198  ( 3.5%)
    v2exl_legitideol_b                               59198  ( 3.5%)
    cinc_a                                               0  ( 0.0%)
    cinc_b                                               0  ( 0.0%)
    rev_potential_a                                  59198  ( 3.5%)
    rev_potential_b                                  59198  ( 3.5%)
    revisionism_distance                            116482  ( 6.8%)
    targets_democracy                                    0  ( 0.0%)
    sidea_revisionist_domestic                       59198  ( 3.5%)
    sidea_nationalist_revisionist_domestic           59198  ( 3.5%)
    sidea_military_support                               0  ( 0.0%)
    sidea_religious_support                         166631  ( 9.8%)
    chrstgenpct_a                                    11941  ( 0.7%)
    islmgenpct_a                                     11941  ( 0.7%)
    islm_dist                                        23862  ( 1.4%)
    chrst_dist                                       23862  ( 1.4%)
